evals.prompt: Use Solo to split this billing migration into API, UI, and independent review lanes. Keep dependencies explicit and bring back verified results.
evals.expected_output: Confirm live scope/tools, write plan scratchpad, create blocked todos, dispatch only disjoint unblocked workers, record child IDs, use idle timers, inspect output/diffs/checks, persist handoffs, and leave final Git integration to operator.
assertions: Implements plan to scratchpad to blockers to bounded workers sequence
Records every returned child process ID and enforces recursive ownership
Uses idle-any for incremental harvest and evidence review before completion
Persists todo comments/scratchpad summary and reserves Git integration for root

evals.prompt: Get a few agents on this bug.
evals.expected_output: Do not maximize agent count; inspect task and current state, keep work single-agent if sequential/overlapping, or first define independent lanes, acceptance checks, ownership, and durable plan before any spawn.
assertions: Treats fan-out as conditional optimization
Inventories project, processes, todos, and locks before dispatch
Requires disjoint ownership and bounded worker briefs
Does not spawn a vague swarm

evals.prompt: Save my standard three-agent review instructions so I can reuse them later; do not launch anyone now.
evals.expected_output: Route to solo-save-prompts, create or propose a scoped placeholder template, and perform no spawning, timer, todo-lock, process-input, or integration side effects.
assertions: Recognizes reusable-text neighboring skill
Routes to solo-save-prompts
Does not spawn agents or create live orchestration state
Keeps live IDs and authority out of template

evals.prompt: $solo-orchestrate-agents split this authentication refactor into independent API, client, and review lanes and bring back verified results.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-orchestrate-agents to confirm live scope and tools, write a plan scratchpad, create blocked todos, dispatch only disjoint unblocked workers with acceptance checks, harvest with idle timers, review evidence, and reserve Git integration for the operator.
assertions: Honors the explicit $solo-orchestrate-agents invocation
Defines disjoint lanes with acceptance checks before any spawn
Uses idle timers to harvest and reviews diffs and output before completion

evals.prompt: Can you get a bunch of agents running in parallel on the different parts of this feature?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-orchestrate-agents, but first inventory processes, todos, and locks, then split the feature into disjoint bounded lanes with ownership and acceptance checks rather than spawning an unscoped swarm.
assertions: Reads casual parallel phrasing as an orchestration trigger
Requires disjoint ownership and bounded worker briefs before dispatch
Does not spawn a vague swarm

evals.prompt: Have two different models each review this pull request independently, then reconcile their findings for me.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-orchestrate-agents to dispatch independent cross-model reviewers over the same diff, keep their lanes isolated, harvest both reviews, and reconcile the evidence into a durable handoff.
assertions: Recognizes independent cross-model review as an orchestration case
Keeps reviewer lanes isolated and harvests each result
Reconciles findings into a persisted handoff

evals.prompt: Spin up a cohort of agents across these three repositories to run the same security audit and collect the results.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-orchestrate-agents to stand up a cross-project cohort with one bounded worker per repo, track child IDs and recursive ownership, harvest with idle timers, and collect verified findings into a durable summary.
assertions: Treats a cross-project cohort as orchestration
Assigns one bounded worker per repo and records child IDs
Collects verified results into a durable handoff

evals.prompt: Split the search and payments work into separate lanes, dispatch a bounded worker per lane, and reconcile their handoffs one lane at a time.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-orchestrate-agents to define disjoint lanes, dispatch one bounded worker per lane, and integrate handoffs one lane at a time after evidence review.
assertions: Keeps orchestration as the primary owner of the mixed request
Delegates the reusable template save to solo-save-prompts
Launches workers only after lanes, ownership, and checks are defined

evals.prompt: Fan out several agents to each stress-test a different module in parallel and report the failures they find.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-orchestrate-agents to assign one worker per module as a disjoint lane, bound each brief with failure-reporting checks, harvest incrementally with idle timers, and persist findings for the operator.
assertions: Maps per-module parallel fan-out to disjoint lanes
Bounds each worker brief with acceptance checks
Harvests incrementally and persists findings

evals.prompt: Delegate these four independent tickets to separate Solo agents and keep them moving until they're all done.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-orchestrate-agents to create blocked todos per ticket, dispatch one bounded worker per independent ticket, enforce recursive ownership of child IDs, harvest with idle timers, and persist per-ticket handoffs.
assertions: Delegates independent tickets as disjoint bounded lanes
Records and recursively owns every child process ID
Tracks each ticket to verified completion with a handoff

evals.prompt: Launch one Claude agent to work through this refactor checklist step by step.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-orchestrate-agents; route to $solo-work-with-agents because this is a single sequential agent session, not multi-agent orchestration.
assertions: Recognizes single-agent sequential work
Routes to solo-work-with-agents
Creates no fan-out or multi-lane orchestration state

evals.prompt: My agent session has gone idle -- check on it and send it a follow-up prompt.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-orchestrate-agents; route to $solo-work-with-agents because inspecting and following up with one owned session is single-agent lifecycle work.
assertions: Recognizes single-session inspect and follow-up
Routes to solo-work-with-agents
Does not spin up additional workers or lanes

evals.prompt: Have an agent make these edits to the shared config file, one change after another.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-orchestrate-agents; route to $solo-work-with-agents because sequential edits to a shared file are single-lane work, not disjoint parallel lanes.
assertions: Recognizes sequential shared-file work
Routes to solo-work-with-agents
Avoids parallel lanes over a shared file

evals.prompt: Research and write me a cited comparison of the top three passkey libraries.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-orchestrate-agents; route to $solo-deep-research because the deliverable is cited, comparative research synthesis.
assertions: Recognizes cited comparative research as the deliverable
Routes to solo-deep-research
Does not treat it as implementation orchestration

evals.prompt: Do exhaustive, cross-checked research on current WebAuthn support and hand me a sourced report.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-orchestrate-agents; route to $solo-deep-research because exhaustive cross-checked research with a cited handoff is a research-synthesis deliverable.
assertions: Recognizes exhaustive cross-checked research synthesis
Routes to solo-deep-research
Reserves multi-agent fan-out judgment to the research skill

evals.prompt: Turn this rollout plan into a set of tracked tasks with priorities and blockers.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-orchestrate-agents; route to $solo-track-todos because this is todo-graph maintenance, not agent dispatch.
assertions: Recognizes todo-graph maintenance
Routes to solo-track-todos
Does not spawn agents or open lanes

evals.prompt: Start all my dev servers and restart the API process.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-orchestrate-agents; route to $solo-run-processes because process lifecycle control is not multi-agent orchestration.
assertions: Distinguishes process control from agent orchestration
Routes to solo-run-processes
Treats multiple processes as unrelated to multi-agent lanes

evals.prompt: I have a mix of Solo tasks to do and I'm not sure which skill handles them.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-orchestrate-agents; route to $fan-solo because skill selection across broad or mixed Solo requests is the router's job.
assertions: Recognizes a broad mixed Solo routing need
Routes to fan-solo
Does not assume orchestration before scope is clear

evals.prompt: Worker A's evidence is reviewed and integrated, worker B is still mid-lane, worker C just went idle but I haven't read its output, and two more lanes are queued. Tidy up and keep the cohort moving.
evals.expected_output: Close or retask harvested worker A now (retask only if a queued lane matches its routed tool/model/effort; otherwise close after durable handoff), leave running worker B alone, inspect worker C's actual output and persist its handoff before any close, and touch only recorded direct children.
assertions: Closes or retasks the harvested worker in the same pass instead of leaving it idle while dispatching new lanes
Refuses to close the worker whose results are not yet reviewed; inspects output and persists handoff first
Does not interrupt or clean the still-running worker and gates every action on recorded ownership
Reuses the idle worker only for a queued lane matching its routed tool, model, and effort; otherwise closes it and routes fresh

evals.prompt: The Sol implementation worker just finished and I merged its diff. The next queued lane is the independent adversarial review of that same change, and the Sol worker is sitting idle—reuse it for the review?
evals.expected_output: No. Consequential independent review requires a different model family from the implementer and a fresh session; close the harvested Sol worker after its handoff is durable and dispatch the review to a different-family reviewer.
assertions: Refuses to reuse the implementer worker to review its own change
Routes the review to a different model family per fleet rules
Closes the harvested implementer worker after durable handoff instead of leaving it idle
Treats reuse as conditional on route fit, not a default
